km.France: ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon於大約1180年與Marie de Montfort在法國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1200年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon于大约1180年与Marie de Montfort在法国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1200年。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon married Marie de Montfort about 1180, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1200.

fr.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon a épousé Marie de Montfort environ 1180, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1200.

de.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon hat um 1180 in Frankreich Marie de Montfort geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1200.

it.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon lui sposato Marie de Montfort circa 1180, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1200.

ja.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençonは約1180年にフランスでMarie de Montfortと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1200年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon(은)는 대략 1180년 프랑스에서 Marie de Montfort(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1200년 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon casou-se com Marie de Montfort aproximadamente 1180, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1200.

ru.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon женился на женщине по имени Marie de Montfort около 1180 г. в местности Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1200 г.

es.lifeSummary: Sire Nicolas II de Barbençon se casó con Marie de Montfort aproximadamente 1180, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1200.

th.1200–Male: ปี1200–ถึงแก่กรรม
